prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "斜下俯卧撑" (decline push-up) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a decline push-up (feet elevated on a knee-height bench/box): hands on the ground shoulder-width apart; feet on top of a bench, the body angled so that the head and torso are lower than the feet (about 30-45 degrees decline); arms bent so chest approaches the floor; body in one straight line from feet to head; this is a harder variant emphasizing the upper chest and shoulders; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: pectoralis major (especially the upper fibers) and anterior deltoid. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: triceps and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "胸大肌" pointing to the pectoralis major (upper fibers); "三角肌前束" pointing to the anterior deltoid; "肱三头肌 / 核心" pointing to the triceps and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
